noveltyJustification: This pass adds the final refined order, split/merge decisions, readiness matrix, and exact first implementation packet from the already validated continuation evidence.
summary: Kept remove-project-cancel-safety first, split background-index work by process boundary, safety-gated sweep/sidecar actions, and marked adapter escalation as benchmark-gated.
Keep correctness/lifecycle before resident-memory work, but split daemon/MCP background-index work and require sidecar ownership metadata before process-sweep sidecar termination.

remove-project-cancel-safety is the exact next implementation packet because it has no upstream dependency and provides the owned task registry/quiescence barrier needed by cancel, shutdown, close, and cache eviction.
daemon-and-mcp-bg-index-task-lifecycle should split into daemon-bg-index-task-registry-shutdown and mcp-bg-index-threadpool-shutdown, or remain one parent with mandatory separate acceptance gates.
Process sweep can implement inventory and classification early, but destructive sidecar actions must wait for sidecar ledger/health ownership metadata and exact-PID/resource confirmation.
project close and registry cache cleanup are ready after remove safety, while adapter lifecycle remains P2 and cannot be escalated without successful-search or fallback-growth measurement.

Recommended refined order: remove-project-cancel-safety; daemon-protocol-cancel-index-surface; daemon-bg-index-task-registry-shutdown; mcp-bg-index-threadpool-shutdown; code-graph-launcher-single-owner-and-orphan-reap; rerank-sidecar-lifecycle; mcp-host-session-process-sweep; project-close-full-release; registry-embedder-cache-lifecycle; adapter-lifecycle-management; code-graph-read-path-friction; no-action F-009 bucket.

research.md should add an iterations 011-015 validation addendum preserving the iteration 009 matrix while adding refined order, split/merge decisions, readiness matrix, first implementation packet, and measurement/safety/test-fixture gates.
If process sweep ships termination behavior before service-specific ownership adapters, it can kill current-session, sibling-session, unknown-liveness, or shared sidecar processes.
mitigation: Ship inventory/dry-run first, block current PID/ancestors/EPERM/unknown owner, require exact PID plus resource identity confirmation, and delegate termination to service policy adapters.

summary: Registry embedder eviction may appear to work while module-level shared.embedder still retains the evicted object.
mitigation: Include fake heavy embedder weakref/finalizer tests that cover registry eviction and shared.embedder update/clear behavior.

summary: The first implementation packet should test remove during active explicit index, load-time auto-index, queued index, and client disconnect; assert bounded cancel/await before registry pop/Project.close and verify post-remove SQLite re-index/search usability.

Follow-on packets need blocking ThreadPool worker fixtures, code-graph orphan/EPERM/symlink DB-dir fixtures, sidecar ledger/unknown-owner fixtures, fake heavy model/client release tests, and successful-search/fallback RSS benchmarks before adapter severity escalation.

hypothesis: Change the first implementation packet away from remove-project-cancel-safety
reason: Iterations 011-014 add dependencies on remove safety; none displace it as the first source-validated prerequisite.

hypothesis: Open adapter-lifecycle-management as a high-priority memory packet before benchmarks
reason: Iteration 014 validated close gaps but added no successful-search or fallback-growth measurement, preserving the P2-bound conclusion from iterations 009-010.

hypothesis: Merge process sweep, sidecar lifecycle, code-graph launcher, and CLI cleanup into one shared reaper
reason: Shared inventory/classification is useful, but each service has different ownership proof and termination rules; a generic reaper hides destructive policy decisions.
